title: Unable to sync preserve-digests dhi.io images
body:
Hi,

I am unable to sync and preserve-digests for [Docker Hardened Images](https://www.docker.com/products/hardened-images/) (dhi.io).  I am using skopeo version 1.20.0.

```
skopeo --debug sync --preserve-digests --all --src docker --dest dir --scoped dhi.io/nginx:1.28-debian12 /tmp/registry
```

Result is always the same regardless of image at dhi.io.

```
Writing manifest list to image destination
DEBU[0010] Trying to use manifest list type application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json...
FATA[0010] Error copying ref "docker://dhi.io/nginx:1.28-debian12": Manifest list must be converted to type "application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json" to be written to destination, but we cannot modify it: "Instructed to preserve digests"
```

Any advice appreciated.

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

This is unexpected, but just from the above I can’t tell how it is failing. As a first step, please actually provide the full `--debug` output.

Example debug output below.

```
skopeo --debug sync --preserve-digests --all --src docker --dest dir --scoped dhi.io/nginx:1.28-alpine3.21 /tmp/registry
```

Thanks.

Can you do a copy without `--preserve-digests`, and compare the top-level objects (e.g. using `skopeo inspect --raw`, on both the source and the destination, please?

I _think_ this was fixed by https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/495 , but it’s hard to tell for sure.

skopeo --debug copy --all docker://dhi.io/nginx:1.28-alpine3.21 dir://tmp/registry/nginx:1.28-alpine3.21

Thank you. Yes, that’s https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/495 , and it will be fixed in the next Skopeo release (or you can build from the main branch).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I am also facing this with a couple Docker Images (both from `docker.io` and `mirror.gcr.io`).

```
Copying image ref 1/1" from="docker://mirror.gcr.io/n8nio/n8n:latest" to="docker://DOCKER.MYDOMAIN.TLD/docker.io/n8nio/n8n:latest"
Error copying ref "docker://mirror.gcr.io/n8nio/n8n:latest": Manifest list must be converted to type "application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json" to be written to destination, but we cannot modify it: "Instructed to preserve digests"

Copying image ref 1/1" from="docker://n8nio/n8n:stable" to="docker://DOCKER.MYDOMAIN.TLD/docker.io/n8nio/n8n:stable"
Error copying ref "docker://n8nio/n8n:stable": Manifest list must be converted to type "application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json" to be written to destination, but we cannot modify it: "Instructed to preserve digests"

Copying image ref 1/1" from="docker://mirror.gcr.io/stalwartlabs/stalwart:latest" to="docker://DOCKER.MYDOMAIN.TLD/docker.io/stalwartlabs/stalwart:latest"
Error copying ref "docker://mirror.gcr.io/stalwartlabs/stalwart:latest": Manifest list must be converted to type "application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json" to be written to destination, but we cannot modify it: "Instructed to preserve digests"
```

Querying the Synced Image Tag Manifest always returns a different Hash.

I only face this with a very few selective Images out of several Hundred though, so it's something of an Edge Case.

EDIT 1: I still see this with Skopeo `1.22.0` 😕.

Note that for whatever Reason the Source is already `application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json`.

EDIT 3: switching to `crane` to Sync / Copy Image seems to do the "Trick" until Skopeo can resolve this Issue 😕:

```
crane copy mirror.gcr.io/stalwartlabs/stalwart:latest DOCKER.MYDOMAIN.TLD/docker.io/stalwartlabs/stalwart:latest
```

> I still see this with Skopeo `1.22.0` 😕.

I’m afraid we ended up releasing 1.22.0 from the 1.21.0 branches; so the fix will land in 1.23. In the meantime, building from `main`, or using a corresponding container image, might be an option.

Uhm OK 🤔.

I actually faced some Issues with `crane` with the `docker.io/library/mariadb` for some weird Reason (`regctl tag ls DOCKER.MYDOMAIN.TLD/docker.io/library/mariadb` would list the Tag, but e.g. `regctl manifest head DOCKER.MYDOMAIN.TLD/docker.io/library/mariadb:10-ubi` would return `404 Not Found`).

Switching to `regctl` and doing `regctl copy` seems to have fixed that, since now Image is synchronized and the Manifest can be accessed using e.g. `regctl manifest head DOCKER.MYDOMAIN.TLD/docker.io/library/mariadb:10-ubi`.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
